title: Internal-External Mail Ratio
spl:
index=security tag=email
| eval ext=if(match(recipient,"@ourdomain\.com$"),0,1)
| timechart span=1h sum(ext) as ext_count, count as tot
| eval ext_ratio=ext_count/tot
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email by All_Email.action, All_Email.src_user span=1h | sort - count
description: Sudden flip to mostly external recipients may indicate data exfil via email or open relay abuse.
value: Sudden flip to mostly external recipients may indicate data exfil via email or open relay abuse.
implementation: Baseline per department; alert when `ext_ratio` >2√ó normal.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Email CIM.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `recipient`, `src_user` with domain classification.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Baseline per department; alert when `ext_ratio` >2√ó normal.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=email
| eval ext=if(match(recipient,"@ourdomain\.com$"),0,1)
| timechart span=1h sum(ext) as ext_count, count as tot
| eval ext_ratio=ext_count/tot
```

Understanding this SPL

**Internal-External Mail Ratio** ‚Äî Sudden flip to mostly external recipients may indicate data exfil via email or open relay abuse.

Documented **Data sources**: `recipient`, `src_user` with domain classification. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Email CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **ext** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets ‚Äî ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **ext_ratio** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email by All_Email.action, All_Email.src_user span=1h | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Internal-External Mail Ratio** ‚Äî Sudden flip to mostly external recipients may indicate data exfil via email or open relay abuse.

Documented **Data sources**: `recipient`, `src_user` with domain classification. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Email CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Email.All_Email` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Area chart (external ratio), Line chart.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
